# Code of Conduct

Capability Fabric is maintained as a professional open-source project. Contributors are
expected to keep discussion focused, respectful, and useful.

## Expected conduct

- Treat contributors with respect.
- Assume good faith, then verify with evidence.
- Keep criticism specific to the work.
- Accept correction when a mistake is shown.
- Avoid personal attacks, harassment, intimidation, and discriminatory language.
- Do not publish private information without explicit permission.

## Scope

This code applies to project spaces, including issues, pull requests, discussions,
documentation, and any public conversation where someone represents the project.

## Reporting

Report conduct concerns to the maintainer through GitHub. If the concern includes a
security issue, use GitHub private vulnerability reporting through the repository
`Security` tab.

Reports should include what happened, where it happened, and any relevant links or
screenshots. Reports will be reviewed with discretion and confidentiality appropriate to
the situation.

## Enforcement

The maintainer may remove comments, close issues, reject contributions, or restrict
participation when conduct undermines the project. Enforcement will be proportional to the
issue and focused on preserving a useful working environment.

## Attribution

This policy is informed by common open-source codes of conduct, including the Contributor
Covenant, but is written specifically for this repository.
